chapter_title.ar: باب صَلَاةِ الْعِيدَيْنِ وَ الْخُطْبَةِ فِيهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 88 – Salāt of the two Eids and the sermon in these two

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/434)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/434)
text: اجْتَمَعَ عِيدَانِ عَلَى عَهْدِ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( صلوات الله عليه ) فَخَطَبَ النَّاسَ ثُمَّ قَالَ هَذَا يَوْمٌ اجْتَمَعَ فِيهِ عِيدَانِ فَمَنْ أَحَبَّ أَنْ يَجْمَعَ مَعَنَا فَلْيَفْعَلْ وَ مَنْ لَمْ يَفْعَلْ فَإِنَّ لَهُ رُخْصَةً يَعْنِي مَنْ كَانَ مُتَنَحِّياً .

en.hubeali: Al-Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al-Washa, from Aban Bin Usman, from Salma,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said, ‘Two Eids were gathered upon the era of Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>. So he<sup>asws</sup> addressed the people, then said: ‘This is a day in which two Eids are gathered. So the one who loves that he should gather along with us, so let him do so, and the one who does not do so, so there is an allowance for him, meaning the one who was laid back’.
en.sarwar: 8. Al-Flusayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Washsha’ from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from Salamah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘During the time of ’Amir al-Mu’minin two ‘Id happened on one day. ’Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, delivered a sermon and said, “This is a day in which two ‘Id have taken place. Those who love can join us and those who do not want to join us, they have permission to do so”, meaning those who keep aside.’”
